cases.steed: a horse, especially one ridden into battle or by a knight
cases.exude: to visibly radiate a feeling or quality without effort
cases.bleak: cold, empty, and without hope or cheer
cases.earnest: sincere and deeply serious in intention
cases.conjecture: a guess formed without solid proof
cases.calloused: having skin toughened and hardened from repeated friction
cases.excruciating: so intense it's almost unbearable to endure
cases.flub: to clumsily mess up a task or performance
cases.pathological: compulsive and irrational to an extreme, almost like a sickness
cases.delinquent: a young person who repeatedly breaks minor laws
cases.loaf: to idle around lazily instead of doing something useful
cases.twinge: a sudden brief stab of pain or emotion
cases.irreconcilable: impossible to bring into agreement with each other
cases.deplorable: shockingly bad and deserving strong disapproval
cases.arbiter: someone with authority to settle a dispute or judge
cases.extol: to praise something enthusiastically and at length
cases.undulation: a smooth, wave-like rising and falling movement
cases.indictment: a formal charge accusing someone of a crime
cases.prattle: to chatter on pointlessly about trivial things
cases.delirious: in a wildly excited or confused state, as if feverish
cases.trifle: to treat something carelessly, without proper seriousness
cases.sophistry: clever arguing that's misleading rather than truly sound
cases.precarious: unstable and likely to collapse or fail at any moment
cases.malaise: a vague, hard-to-pin-down feeling of unease or sickness
cases.deific: having the qualities or status of a god
cases.craven: utterly and shamefully lacking in courage
cases.abhor: to hate something with intense disgust
cases.solicit: to actively ask for or seek something from someone
cases.scrutinize: to inspect something very closely and critically
cases.obstinate: stubbornly unwilling to change one's mind
cases.wry: dryly humorous, often with a twisted, knowing expression
cases.scoff: to mock or dismiss something with contempt
cases.refrain: to stop yourself from doing something
cases.detestable: so unpleasant it inspires strong dislike
cases.exquisite: extremely beautiful and delicately, finely made
cases.intricate: having many finely detailed, complicated parts
cases.frazzled: worn out and on edge from stress or exhaustion
cases.unfathomable: impossible to fully grasp or understand
cases.heed: to pay close attention to advice or a warning
cases.ostentatious: showy in a way meant to impress or flaunt wealth
cases.conceited: having an inflated sense of one's own importance
cases.harem: a group of women associated with one man historically
cases.curtail: to cut something short or reduce it
cases.indecency: behavior that offends accepted standards of modesty
cases.insufferable: so obnoxious it's unbearable to be around
cases.letch: a man who ogles or behaves lustfully toward women
cases.casanova: a man famous for seducing many women
cases.narcissist: someone excessively absorbed in admiring themselves
cases.mutineer: someone who revolts against their ship's leadership
cases.disinclined: reluctant or unwilling to do something
cases.acquiesce: to reluctantly go along with something without objecting
cases.pretentious: putting on airs of importance one doesn't deserve
cases.incredulous: unable or unwilling to believe something
cases.quack: someone who falsely poses as a qualified doctor
cases.oblivious: completely unaware of what's going on around you
cases.promiscuous: having casual sexual relationships with many partners
cases.heathen: someone regarded as uncivilized or without religion
cases.gouge: to dig or force something out roughly
cases.whimper: to make small, weak, pitiful crying sounds
cases.addled: confused and unable to think straight
cases.lambency: a soft, gentle glow or radiance
cases.deign: to lower oneself to do something seen as beneath you
cases.transgression: a violation of a moral rule or boundary
cases.sluggish: moving slowly, with little energy or vigor
cases.ominous: giving an unsettling sense that something bad is coming
cases.harbor: to secretly hold onto a feeling or intention
cases.wallow: to indulge excessively in a miserable feeling
cases.progenitor: the original ancestor or founder of a lineage
cases.leer: to look at someone with a sly, lustful stare
cases.nuptial: relating to a wedding or marriage ceremony
cases.shackle: a restraint that binds and limits someone's freedom
cases.gullible: too easily tricked or deceived into believing things
cases.coulis: a smooth, thick sauce made from pureed fruit or vegetables
cases.timbale: food shaped and cooked in a small drum-like mold
cases.freebase: to purify a drug by heating it for smoking
cases.mull: to think something over slowly and carefully
cases.anachronism: something that seems out of place for its time period
cases.dystopia: an imagined society that is deeply oppressive and grim
cases.utopian: relating to an ideal, perfect imagined society
cases.prehensile: adapted for grasping or holding onto things
cases.jovial: warmly cheerful and full of good humor
cases.incinerate: to burn something completely to ash
cases.euphemism: a mild, polite word substituted for a harsh one
cases.infallible: never wrong, incapable of making a mistake
cases.reclusive: preferring to avoid other people and stay isolated
cases.apprehensive: anxiously worried about something that might happen
cases.reminiscent: calling to mind something similar from before
cases.mannerism: a distinctive habitual gesture or way someone behaves
cases.evoke: to bring a feeling or memory vividly to mind
cases.anticlimactic: disappointingly unexciting after a big build-up
cases.feign: to pretend to feel or experience something
cases.trepidation: a fearful, anxious feeling about what's coming
cases.respite: a brief period of rest from something difficult
cases.obliterate: to destroy something so completely nothing remains
cases.cacophonous: full of harsh, clashing, unpleasant noise
cases.abide by: to follow and accept a rule or decision
cases.let sleeping dogs lie: to avoid stirring up a problem that's currently quiet
cases.idiosyncrasy: a quirky habit peculiar to one particular person
cases.languish: to weaken or waste away from being neglected
cases.benchwarmer: a substitute player who rarely gets into the game
cases.crescendo: a gradual build to a peak of loudness or intensity
cases.apathetic: showing no interest or emotional investment in anything
cases.banter: light, playful teasing conversation between people
cases.deadpan: delivered with a serious face despite being a joke
cases.ogle: to stare at someone with obvious desire
cases.voluptuous: having a full, curvy, sensuous figure
cases.clad: dressed or covered in a particular kind of clothing
cases.reverberate: to echo repeatedly through a space
cases.patronize: to be a regular paying customer of a business
cases.ruse: a trick meant to deceive someone
cases.facade: a false front that hides someone's true nature
cases.nonchalant: casually unconcerned, as if nothing bothers you
cases.bombshell: a piece of news that comes as a shocking surprise
cases.reprimand: to formally scold someone for doing wrong
cases.pillage: to violently plunder and loot, especially during war
cases.nitty-gritty: the essential, practical core details of a matter
cases.epiphany: a sudden flash of understanding or insight
cases.in tandem: together at the same time, in coordination
cases.furrow: to wrinkle one's forehead in thought or worry
cases.dismissive: showing that you consider something not worth attention
cases.convoluted: needlessly complicated and hard to follow
cases.scruff: the loose skin and short hair at the back of the neck
cases.ponder: to think about something slowly and carefully
cases.radicalization: the process of adopting increasingly extreme beliefs
cases.sway: to influence or gradually persuade someone's opinion
